Our streamlined 6-step process ensures you get maximum value from your swimwear sampling project with clear deliverables at each stage.

We analyze your tech pack and designs to establish the foundational fit block, target market measurements, and discuss key swim-specific construction techniques (e.g., flatlock stitching, lining, elastic application).

We present and source approved swim-specific fabrics (e.g., nylon-spandex blends, recycled materials) and critical trims (e.g., chlorine-resistant elastics, bust cups, hardware) based on your performance and aesthetic needs.

Our technicians draft the first precision swimwear pattern, accounting for fabric stretch and recovery. A preliminary muslin or digital mock-up is often created to validate the fit on a form before cutting the final fabric.

The first physical sample is constructed. We conduct a rigorous internal fit session on a live model or mannequin, checking for garment tension, gapping, and mobility to identify any construction adjustments.

The sample undergoes final finishing (e.g., hook & eye application, label sewing) and a stringent quality inspection for stitching integrity, seam flatness, and trim placement against your specifications.

You receive the sample for evaluation. We consolidate your feedback on fit, fabric hand-feel, and overall aesthetic to refine the prototype and present a clear roadmap to production, including grading rules for your size set.
What is typically included in your sampling service, and what is the cost?

Our sampling service is a comprehensive package that includes pattern engineering, sourcing of approved fabrics and trims, construction of one physical prototype, and an internal fit analysis. The cost is project-specific and varies based on the design's complexity, chosen materials, and trim details. We provide a transparent quote after our initial consultation to align on the scope.
What is the typical turnaround time for a sample?

The standard timeline for a first prototype is 2-3 weeks. This period includes material sourcing, pattern development, and meticulous construction. We can discuss expedited options for urgent projects, and timelines are always confirmed upfront.
Do I need to provide a complete tech pack, or can you work from my design concept?

We can work with both. A detailed tech pack allows for the most accurate and efficient process. However, if you have a design concept or inspiration, our team can help you develop it into a production-ready specification, including fit, fabric, and construction recommendations.
How do you handle fit and sizing for samples?

We begin by aligning on a base fit block and specific grade rules for your target market. The first sample is constructed to these precise specifications and is evaluated internally on a standard dress form. We highly recommend you also conduct your own fit session on a live model that matches your target customer's measurements.
Can you source specific or sustainable fabrics for my sample?

Absolutely. We have established relationships with mills offering a wide range of performance and sustainable fabrics (e.g., recycled nylon, OEKO-TEX® certified materials). We will curate options based on your aesthetic, performance, and sustainability goals and provide swatches for your approval.
What happens if the sample requires adjustments after delivery?

Iteration is a core part of the sampling process. We include one round of detailed feedback consolidation after you receive your first sample. We'll analyze your notes on fit and construction and present a clear plan for revisions, including any associated costs and timeline for a second prototype if needed.
How does the sampling process transition to bulk production?

The final, approved sample serves as the 'gold standard' for production. We use the refined patterns, confirmed materials, and construction details from the sampling phase to create a seamless production roadmap, providing you with a firm quotation for your bulk order based on your required quantities.
